summary: Import-time remote code execution via anonymous paste-service fetch and eval
details: On require(), index.js invokes getPlugin() at module top level, which performs an HTTP GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QBRMI (a public anonymous JSON paste service) and passes the response's `model` field directly to eval(). Any consumer importing this package executes whatever JavaScript the attacker currently hosts at that mutable, anonymous endpoint — no integrity check, no version pinning. The package compounds this with namespace-abuse: it advertises itself as 'get necessory dependencies' (a dependency installer), but the shipped code is a copy of koa-compose middleware (keywords: koa, middleware, compose; exports a `compose` function) bundled with the remote-eval payload. The name/description/keyword incoherence is consistent with luring imports from developers expecting either a dependency-path utility or koa middleware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
